items.preprocessing: 6/7 - Downsampling is implemented with a configurable target_size and correct scale computation, suitable for very large images. Gaussian blur is applied with configurable kernel and sigma. Background subtraction is implemented via morphological opening with a configurable structuring element. There is also normalization and optional CLAHE. The only minor gap is the lack of explicit performance tuning for 30000×30000 images beyond simple resize, but algorithmically the pipeline is complete.
items.coarse_detection: 5/7 - Largest-contour-based detection with minEnclosingCircle is correctly implemented (detect_by_largest_contour and find_contours using Canny). Additional Hough and moment-based methods exist. However, the main detect method is truncated and the combination logic (and potential fallback between methods) is not visible, and there is no explicit code shown to map the detected circle back to original-resolution coordinates (though scale is tracked in main). Overall solid but not fully complete/visible.
items.fine_detection: 5/7 - Radial scanning along multiple angles (num_rays) is implemented, using Sobel gradients and magnitude normalization. There is a dual-edge-like selection via _detect_dual_edge that searches for a strong positive gradient near the maximum, which matches the dual-edge/transition idea. However, sub-pixel edge refinement is not present, and the fine_detect wrapper and integration with robust statistics are truncated, so the full fine detection pipeline is incomplete in the provided code.
items.statistical_analysis: 2/7 - The RobustStatisticalAnalysis class and AnalysisResult dataclass are declared, but the implementation is truncated before any MAD computation, sigma-based rejection, or final robust radius/center estimation logic is visible. Based on what is shown, MAD and 3-sigma usage are only implied by parameter names, not actually implemented in the visible code.
items.mask_generation: 4/7 - Circular and ring mask generation are well implemented with optional smooth edges via a sigmoid function. There is an attempt at chord detection based on angular gaps between edge points and a MAD-based criterion, and an apply_chord_clipping method stub that aims to crop based on chord positions. However, apply_chord_clipping is truncated, and there is no visible implementation of chord midpoints, high-intensity point detection, or explicit outlier coordinate identification tied into feature detection. Thus mask creation is solid, chord logic is partial, and feature detection/cropping is incomplete.

eval_report.comment: Good — solid implementation with minor gaps.
